## What you need to do next

### 1. Create the EAS project

```bash
cd expo
bun x eas init
```

This will create an EAS project. Then add the project ID to your `expo/.env`:

```bash
EXPO_PUBLIC_EAS_PROJECT_ID=your-actual-project-id
```

### 2. Apple App Store

- Register at [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com) ($99/year)
- Create an app in App Store Connect with bundle ID `com.ivxholdings.app`
- Create an App Store Connect API key and download the `.p8` file
- Place it at `expo/keys/asc-api-key.p8`
- Add these to `expo/.env`:

```bash
EXPO_PUBLIC_ASC_APP_ID=your-app-id
EXPO_PUBLIC_ASC_ISSUER_ID=your-issuer-id
EXPO_PUBLIC_ASC_KEY_ID=your-key-id
```

### 3. Google Play

- Register at [play.google.com/console](https://play.google.com/console) ($25 one-time)
- Create an app with package `com.ivxholdings.app`
- Create a Google Play service account and download `google-service-account.json`
- Place it at `expo/keys/google-service-account.json`

### 4. Build and submit

Internal test builds:

```bash
cd expo
bun x eas build --profile development --platform ios
bun x eas build --profile development --platform android
```

Production store submission:

```bash
cd expo
bun x eas build --profile production --platform ios --auto-submit
bun x eas build --profile production --platform android --auto-submit
```

The `production` profile uses `autoIncrement: true` so version numbers and build numbers will be managed automatically by EAS.

## iOS vs Android

Your Expo app is one React Native codebase. When you build with EAS, the **same code** produces both the iOS and Android apps. They will match visually and functionally. You do not need a separate Swift app unless you want to later replace the iOS version with native `ios-ivx/`.
